name: Cat Cow Stretch
instructions: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders.
Inhale and gently arch your back, lifting chest and tailbone.
Exhale and round your spine, letting your head relax.
Move slowly for 6 to 8 breaths.
summary: Inhale to open the chest, then exhale to gently round the back. Keep wrists under shoulders, use a small range, and move with the breath.
focus: spine flow ? back release ? low intensity
safety: Reduce the range if wrists or lower back feel uncomfortable. Avoid collapsing the low back or holding your breath.

name: Child Pose Breathing
instructions: Kneel and sit hips toward heels.
Fold forward and rest your arms comfortably.
Breathe into your ribs and back.
Stay for 30 to 60 seconds.
summary: Let the back soften slowly with each exhale. Lower the forehead, relax neck and shoulders, and stay for about 6 breaths.
focus: breath release ? lower back comfort ? recovery
safety: Place a towel under the knees if needed. Widen the knees if the abdomen feels compressed.

name: Neck Release
instructions: Sit tall and relax your shoulders.
Tilt one ear toward the shoulder without forcing.
Hold for a few breaths, then switch sides.
summary: Keep the chin slightly tucked and let the shoulders soften as you exhale. Pause on both sides without pulling.
focus: neck and shoulder release ? seated option ? very low intensity
safety: Do not pull the head with your hand. Stop if you feel dizziness, numbness, tingling, or sharp pain.

name: Gentle Hip Rock
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent.
Let knees rock slowly side to side.
Keep the range small and comfortable.
summary: Exhale as the knees slowly fall to one side. Keep the range comfortable, the low back grounded, and alternate sides.
focus: hip release ? bloating-friendly ? lower back comfort
safety: Keep the movement small. Stop if lower back pain or abdominal pressure becomes noticeable.

name: Seated Spinal Twist
instructions: Sit tall with legs crossed or extended comfortably.
Inhale to lengthen your spine.
Exhale and rotate gently from the ribs.
Return to center and switch sides.
summary: Inhale to lengthen the spine, then exhale into a gentle rotation. Keep the sitting bones grounded and avoid chasing range.
focus: trunk rotation ? bloating support ? posture reset
safety: Avoid fast twisting. Skip during pregnancy, acute low back pain, or clear abdominal pain.

name: Belly Breathing
instructions: Sit or lie down comfortably.
Place one hand on chest and one on belly.
Inhale slowly and let the belly soften.
Exhale longer than the inhale.
summary: Let the belly rise naturally on the inhale, then make the exhale slightly longer. Relax the shoulders and keep the rhythm slow.
safety: If deeper breathing makes you dizzy, return to natural breathing without forcing depth.

name: Standing Side Stretch
instructions: Stand with feet grounded and knees soft.
Reach one arm overhead.
Lean gently to the opposite side.
Breathe into the side ribs, then switch sides.
summary: Inhale to lengthen the side body, then exhale into a gentle side bend. Keep knees soft and shoulders away from ears.
focus: side-body stretch ? light standing movement ? body wake-up
safety: Do not compress the low back or lean backward. Sit down and do the same stretch if you feel dizzy.

name: Seated Forward Fold
instructions: Sit with legs extended and knees slightly soft.
Inhale to lengthen your spine.
Exhale and fold only as far as comfortable.
Rest hands on legs or the floor.
summary: Inhale to lengthen the back, then fold only as far as comfortable. Bend the knees and stop at an easy range.
focus: hamstring stretch ? lower back release ? quiet finish
safety: Do not force toward the feet. Reduce the range or skip if low back or sciatic discomfort appears.

name: Butterfly Stretch
instructions: Sit tall and bring soles of feet together.
Let knees open without pushing them down.
Hold ankles or rest hands on thighs.
Breathe calmly for 30 to 60 seconds.
summary: Sit tall and let the hips open naturally. Keep the knees relaxed, the back long, and the breath even.
focus: hip opening ? pelvic-area release ? gentle hold
safety: Support the thighs with pillows if knees or hips feel uncomfortable. Avoid bouncing.

name: Legs Up The Wall
instructions: Lie on your back near a wall.
Rest legs upward against the wall.
Relax arms by your sides.
Breathe naturally for 1 to 3 minutes.
summary: Rest the legs against the wall and breathe naturally. Keep shoulders relaxed and the low back supported.
safety: Skip if dizziness, eye-pressure concerns, or strong discomfort appear. Roll to one side before sitting up.

name: Shoulder Rolls
instructions: Sit or stand tall with arms relaxed.
Roll shoulders slowly up, back, and down.
Keep jaw and neck soft.
Repeat 6 to 10 circles, then reverse.
summary: Roll the shoulders slowly back and down. Keep the chin lightly tucked and let the neck stay soft.
focus: neck and shoulder ease ? seated or standing ? work-break friendly
safety: Do not shrug with force. Reduce the range or stop if shoulder pain appears.

name: Slow Mindful Walk
instructions: Walk slowly indoors or outdoors at an easy pace.
Let arms swing naturally.
Keep breathing steady.
Stop before fatigue builds.
summary: Walk slowly and let breath and steps settle into a steady rhythm. Keep shoulders relaxed and stop before fatigue builds.
safety: Shorten the time if menstrual fatigue or dizziness appears. Keep it easy, not a training push.
